# Contributing

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-05 -->

Thanks for helping improve `contentful.rb`.

## Prerequisites

| Tool | Version | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Ruby | 3.2 – 3.4 (see devcontainer) | Managed via rbenv or the included dev container |
| Bundler | 2.3.26 | Pinned in `postCreateCommand`; install with `gem install bundler:2.3.26` |
| Docker + Dev Container CLI | Latest | Required for the recommended contributor workflow |

The fastest path to a working environment is the included dev container — it installs all dependencies automatically. You can also run natively if you have a supported Ruby version installed.

## Getting Started

### Recommended: Dev Container

**VS Code:**
1. Install the [Dev Containers extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers)
2. Open the repo in VS Code → run `Dev Containers: Reopen in Container`
3. Wait for the container build and post-create setup to finish (`bundle _2.3.26_ install`)

**Terminal / other editors:**
```bash
# Install the Dev Container CLI
npm install -g @devcontainers/cli@0

# Start the container
devcontainer up --workspace-folder .
devcontainer exec --workspace-folder . bash
```

### Native (no Docker)

```bash
# Clone and install
git clone git@github.com:contentful/contentful.rb.git
cd contentful.rb
gem install bundler:2.3.26
bundle _2.3.26_ install

# Verify
bundle exec rake rspec_rubocop    # source: Rakefile → task rspec_rubocop
```

## Development Workflow

```bash
# Run all tests and linting (primary check — same as CI)
bundle exec rake rspec_rubocop    # source: Rakefile → task rspec_rubocop

# Watch mode (re-runs specs, rubocop, and yard on file changes)
bundle exec guard                 # source: Guardfile
```

## Commands

### Testing

```bash
bundle exec rake spec             # source: Rakefile → RSpec::Core::RakeTask
bundle exec rspec                 # equivalent — runs the full spec suite
bundle exec rspec spec/client_configuration_spec.rb   # run a single spec file
```

### Linting

```bash
bundle exec rake rubocop          # source: Rakefile → RuboCop::RakeTask
bundle exec rubocop               # equivalent
bundle exec rubocop --auto-correct   # apply safe autocorrections
```

### Combined (what CI runs)

```bash
bundle exec rake rspec_rubocop    # source: Rakefile → task rspec_rubocop (:spec + :rubocop)
```

### Documentation

```bash
bundle exec yard doc              # source: .yardopts; excludes private methods
```

### Release

```bash
bundle exec rake release          # source: Rakefile → Gem::Tasks; pushes commit, tags, and publishes to RubyGems
```

## Testing

- **Framework:** RSpec (~> 3) with `--color --format documentation` (see `.rspec`)
- **Location:** `spec/` — one spec file per `lib/contentful/*.rb` file
- **HTTP mocking:** VCR + WebMock — cassettes in `spec/fixtures/vcr_cassettes/`; JSON fixtures in `spec/fixtures/json_responses/`
- **Coverage:** SimpleCov — runs automatically with `rake spec`
- **Run all:** `bundle exec rake spec`
- **Run single file:** `bundle exec rspec spec/<file>_spec.rb`
- **Watch mode:** `bundle exec guard` (runs RSpec on file changes)

No VCR cassette re-recording is needed for most changes. Add new JSON fixtures in `spec/fixtures/json_responses/` for new resource types or response shapes.

## Code Style & Conventions

- **Style:** RuboCop (~> 1.60) — config in `.rubocop.yml`, overrides in `.rubocop_todo.yml`
- **Scope:** Applied to `lib/` only; `spec/`, `examples/`, `Gemfile`, `Rakefile`, and `contentful.gemspec` are excluded (see `.rubocop.yml → AllCops.Exclude`)
- **Key rules:** `Encoding` is disabled; `Metrics/ClassLength` max is 280; `Metrics/ParameterLists` max is 6
- **Key style:** `Style/SignalException` uses semantic style (`raise` vs `fail`)
- **Field accessor naming:** All field accessors use `snake_case` by default. The `use_camel_case: true` client option exists for isomorphic apps — don't assume either style in library internals
- **Frozen string literals:** `# frozen_string_literal: true` at top of each `lib/` file

## Commit Convention

This repo uses a mix of conventional and freeform commits. CI does not enforce a commit format.

Prefer descriptive commit messages:
```
feat: add taxonomy endpoint support
fix: handle nil includes when reuse_entries is true
chore: bump rubocop to 1.60.2
docs: update configuration table
```

Recent PRs have adopted `type: description [TICKET]` format — matching is appreciated but not required.

## Branch Strategy

- `master` — main branch; CI runs on push and pull request to `master`
- Feature branches — create from `master`, open a PR back to `master`
- No protected branch policy beyond CI checks

## Release Process

See [RELEASE.md](./RELEASE.md) and the Operational Knowledge section in [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md) for the full manual release workflow. In short:

1. Update `lib/contentful/version.rb` (semver)
2. Update `CHANGELOG.md` — move Unreleased entries to the new version header
3. Commit both files: `git commit -m "Bump to version X.Y.Z"`
4. Run `bundle exec rake release` — pushes, tags, publishes to RubyGems

## Pull Requests

1. Fork or branch from `master`
2. Run `bundle exec rake rspec_rubocop` and make sure it's green
3. Open a PR with a short summary of the change and any relevant context
4. All PRs require CI to pass (`test-and-lint` matrix across Ruby 3.2, 3.3, 3.4)
5. Maintainer review required before merge

## CI/CD

| Job | Trigger | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `test-and-lint` | Push to `master` / PR to `master` | Runs `bundle exec rake rspec_rubocop` inside dev container across Ruby 3.2, 3.3, 3.4 matrix |
| CodeQL | Push / PR | Static security analysis |

CI config: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

## Sensitive Files

| Path | Why sensitive |
|---|---|
| `lib/contentful/version.rb` | Version bumps must be part of a release commit — do not modify independently |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Must stay in sync with version bumps; format follows [Keep a Changelog](http://keepachangelog.com/) |
| `.rubocop_todo.yml` | Auto-generated by `rubocop --auto-gen-config` — do not hand-edit |
| `spec/fixtures/vcr_cassettes/` | VCR cassette recordings — regenerate by deleting and re-running specs with live API access |
